patcher.title: Game Modifications
patcher.description: Configure standalone patches to apply directly to the Terraria executable.

modal.title: Patching Terraria
modal.stepRestorePrompt: A backup (Terraria.exe.bak) was found. Would you like to restore it before patching?
modal.stepRestoreWarning: Warning: The current version ({{exeVersion}}) differs from the backup version ({{bakVersion}}). Restoring an older backup is not recommended.
modal.stepBackupPrompt: Would you like to create a backup of the current executable before applying new patches?

errors.noPath: No Terraria path configured. Go to Config to set it.
errors.doublePatch: Both Terraria.exe and the backup are ALREADY PATCHED. Please verify your game files on Steam to restore a clean version before patching again!

tabsDescriptions.combat: Modifications to combat mechanics and negative effects.
tabsDescriptions.cheats: Features that strongly alter game balance.
tabsDescriptions.buffs: Select buffs that will be permanently active for your character.

time.label: Display Time
time.desc: Shows the current in-game time accurately without needing a watch.

social.label: Functional Social Slots
social.desc: Accessories placed in social slots will grant their stats and effects.

range.label: Max Crafting Range
range.desc: Access all nearby crafting stations without having to stand right next to them.

pylon.label: Pylon Everywhere (No Restrictions)
pylon.desc: Use pylons regardless of NPC happiness or biome requirements.

angler.label: Remove Angler Quest Per Day Limit
angler.desc: Complete as many fishing quests as you want per in-game day.
angler.warningDesc: This mod is reported to break Steam achievements for the Angler. It will still allow you to get in-game achievements though.

rod.label: Remove Rod of Discord Debuff
rod.desc: Teleport infinitely without taking damage from Chaos State.

potion.label: Remove Potion Sickness Debuff
potion.desc: Consume healing potions anytime without having to wait for cooldowns.

mana.label: Remove Mana Costs
mana.desc: Magic weapons and tools will not consume your mana.

drowning.label: Remove Drowning
drowning.desc: Breathe underwater infinitely without taking damage.

ohk.label: One Hit Kill
ohk.desc: Instantly kill any enemy or boss with a single attack.

ammo.label: Infinite Ammo
ammo.desc: Ranged weapons will never consume ammunition.

wings.label: Permanent Stardust Wings + Infinite Up Time
wings.desc: Grants infinite flight time and optimal wing performance permanently.

healing.desc: Adjust the effectiveness of lifesteal items and armors.

spawning.desc: Modify the spawn rates of specific enemies.
spawning.voodooDesc: Controls how often a regular Demon is replaced by a Voodoo Demon. For example, 50% means 1 out of 2 demons will be a Voodoo Demon.

lootFeature.desc: Configure item drops and boss reward modifications.
lootFeature.treasureBags: Treasure Bags always drop full loot table
lootFeature.treasureBagsDesc: Opening a treasure bag will guarantee dropping every possible item instead of random selections.

patcher.pathAlert: Make sure your Terraria path is correctly set in the Config page before patching.

home.description: A powerful desktop tool for patching and customizing your Terraria experience. Apply patches, manage plugins, and configure your setup — all in one place.

patcher.title: Patcher
patcher.desc: Apply and manage game patches with ease.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.step1: Navigate to the Patcher page to apply your patches.
quickStart.step2: Head to Plugins to enable your favorite mods.
quickStart.step3: Adjust your preferences in the Config page.

inspiration.title: Inspiration & Credits
inspiration.p1: This project is heavily inspired by and based on an existing project also called <1>Terraria Patcher</1>, created by the wonderful developer Doug Benham.
inspiration.p2: We want to give a massive thank you to Doug for his amazing work laying the foundation for Terraria patching. His original tool is built 100% for Windows. You can find his original repository here:
inspiration.p3: Our project aims to build upon his excellent foundation by introducing a modern interface and new capabilities.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.p1: While the original Patcher is Windows-only, our goal is to bring this tool to everyone. Our platform is designed from the ground up to run on <1>Windows, Linux, and macOS</1>.
vision.note: <0>Note:</0> Although the application itself runs across all operating systems seamlessly, the actual Terraria patching functionality for Linux and macOS is still under development and not yet fully supported.
vision.p3: You can follow our progress and help build the project through open source at:

stack.title: Technology Stack
stack.desc: To achieve our cross-platform goals with a beautiful, responsive user interface, we utilized the following modern web technologies:

emptyState.title: No Recent Changes
emptyState.desc: The changelog will display the history of versions and updates made to the application.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.subtitle: Browse, enable, and configure plugins
plugins.enableSupportDesc: Allows custom functionality by loading localized C# scripts.

emptyState.title: No plugins loaded
emptyState.desc: This section lists all available plugins located natively in the resources/plugins dir.

ini.title: Plugins.ini Editor
ini.subtitle: Edit only the settings generated by PluginLoader and your plugins.
ini.pathUnknown: Plugins.ini path will appear here after loading.
ini.deleteConfirm: Delete Plugins.ini? This only removes the generated plugin settings file.
ini.hotkeyHint: Use key names from the plugin format (examples: R, F, OemSemicolon).
ini.emptyDesc: Plugins.ini exists but currently has no generated sections.

errors.noTerrariaPathDesc: Set the Terraria.exe path in the Config page before using the Plugins.ini editor.

messages.notFoundDesc: The file was not found yet. First apply the plugin patch, then run Terraria once so PluginLoader can generate Plugins.ini.

gameDirectory.title: Game Directory
gameDirectory.desc: Configure the installation path for Terraria.
gameDirectory.label: Terraria Executable Location
gameDirectory.help: The patcher needs to locate your main game executable to apply modifications.

appPreferences.title: App Preferences
appPreferences.pluginDesc: Load third-party patches from the <1>\Plugins\*.cs</1> directory.

profile.title: Settings Profile
profile.desc: Export or import your patch selections and app settings as a JSON file.
profile.includes: Includes patch options, selected persistent buffs, active plugins, plugin support, language, and Terraria path.
